rationale: 5% relative AUC drop is operationally significant for CRM targeting
Retention offers become unprofitable if too many non-churners are targeted
Campaign ROI requires incremental uplift in targeted deciles
Negative ATE means the campaign is hurting response rate
RMSE above naive baseline (2.172) signals model deterioration
SMAPE above 70% indicates forecast is unusable for replenishment
Low silhouette means segments are not well-separated; CRM lists lose value
Large Champions shrinkage indicates customer base deterioration or data issue
Below 0.006 approaches content-based baseline; personalisation value is lost
Flag rate spike indicates a data anomaly or model degradation; overwhelms risk ops
Flag rate too low means model stopped scoring or data feed is missing
